questions.prompt: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is (9x − 560)°. The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is (−18x + w)°. What is the value of w?
questions.rationale: All acute angles are equal; all obtuse angles are equal. If an acute angle is (9x − 560)°, then an obtuse angle is 180 − (9x − 560) = −9x + 740. The given sum is (9x − 560) + 3(−9x + 740) = −18x + 1660, which matches −18x + w, so w = 1660.

questions.prompt: Triangle ABC is similar to triangle XYZ, where A, B, and C correspond to X, Y, and Z, respectively. In triangle ABC, the length of AB is 170 and the length of BC is 850. In triangle XYZ, the length of YZ is 60. What is the length of XY?
questions.rationale: By similarity, AB ↔ XY and BC ↔ YZ, so XY/AB = YZ/BC = 60/850. Thus XY = 170·(60/850) = 12.

questions.prompt: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 54°, the measure of angle B is 90°, and the measure of angle C is (k/2)°. What is the value of k?
In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 50°. If triangle ABC is isosceles, which of the following is NOT a possible measure of angle B?
questions.rationale: If B = 100°, then C = 180 − (50 + 100) = 30°, giving angles 50°, 100°, 30° with no two equal, so it can’t be isosceles. The other choices can produce two equal angles.

questions.prompt: Each side of equilateral triangle S is multiplied by a scale factor of k to create equilateral triangle T. The length of each side of triangle T is greater than the length of each side of triangle S. Which of the following could be the value of k?
questions.rationale: To make triangle T larger, the scale factor must be greater than 1. Only 29/28 is greater than 1.

questions.prompt: In convex pentagon ABCDE, segment AB is parallel to segment DE. The measure of angle B is 139° and the measure of angle D is 174°. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle C?
questions.rationale: Extend AB at B to form an exterior angle of 180 − 139 = 41°. Using AB ∥ DE, that 41° transfers as an alternate interior angle with a transversal through C. Angle D = 174° gives an adjacent exterior angle of 180 − 174 = 6°. Angle C is the sum of those two angles: 41 + 6 = 47.

questions.prompt: Triangles PQR and LMN are graphed in the xy-plane. Triangle PQR has vertices P, Q, and R at (4, 5), (4, 7), and (6, 5), respectively. Triangle LMN has vertices L, M, and N at (4, 5), (4, 7 + k), and (6 + k, 5), respectively, where k is a positive constant. If the measure of ∠Q is t°, what is the measure of ∠N?
questions.rationale: Triangle PQR is a right isosceles triangle, so ∠Q = 45°. The construction of LMN preserves a right isosceles shape, so ∠N = 45° as well. Since t = 45°, the expression equal to 45° is 90 − t.

questions.prompt: In triangle JKL, the measures of ∠K and ∠L are each 48°. What is the measure of ∠J, in degrees? (Disregard the degree symbol when entering your answer.)
In right triangle ABC, angle C is the right angle and BC = 162. Point D on side AB is connected to point E on side AC such that DE is parallel to BC and CE = 2AE. What is the length of DE?
questions.rationale: Since DE ∥ BC, triangles ADE and ABC are similar. On AC, AE + CE = AC and CE = 2AE, so AC = 3AE, hence AE/AC = 1/3. Therefore DE/BC = 1/3, so DE = (1/3)·162 = 54.

questions.prompt: In triangle XYZ, angle Y is a right angle. Point P lies on XZ, and point Q lies on YZ such that PQ is parallel to XY. If the measure of angle XZY is 63°, what is the measure, in degrees, of angle XPQ?
questions.rationale: With PQ ∥ XY and ∠Y = 90°, angle ZPQ equals 90° − ∠XZY = 90 − 63 = 27° (complementary). Angle XPQ is supplementary to angle ZPQ along line XP, so ∠XPQ = 180 − 27 = 153.
